server_like.c(3) Library Functions Manual server_like.c(3)

NAME

server_like.c - Unified lifecycle for server-like modes (server, discovery-service).

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define KEYBOARD_QUEUE_SIZE 256

Functions


tcp_server_t * session_server_like_get_tcp_server (void)
asciichat_mdns_t * session_server_like_get_mdns_ctx (void)
nat_upnp_context_t * session_server_like_get_upnp_ctx (void)
websocket_server_t * session_server_like_get_websocket_server (void)
asciichat_error_t session_server_like_mdns_advertise (const char *name, const char *type, uint16_t port, const char **txt, size_t txt_count)
asciichat_error_t session_server_like_handshake (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport)
asciichat_error_t session_server_like_run (const session_server_like_config_t *config)

Detailed Description

Unified lifecycle for server-like modes (server, discovery-service).

Owns TCP server, WebSocket server, mDNS, UPnP, status screen, keyboard, and signal handling. Modes plug in via callbacks.

Definition in file server_like.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define KEYBOARD_QUEUE_SIZE 256

Definition at line 64 of file server_like.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_mdns_t * session_server_like_get_mdns_ctx (void )

Get the mDNS context owned by server_like.

Available after mDNS init completes (i.e., during init_fn and after). Use for deferred advertisement when service_name is not known at config time.

Returns

Pointer to mDNS context, or NULL if not initialized or disabled

Definition at line 256 of file server_like.c.

256                                                          {
257   return g_mdns_ctx;
258 }








tcp_server_t * session_server_like_get_tcp_server (void
  )


Get the TCP server instance owned by server_like.




Available after tcp_server_init() completes (i.e., during
    init_fn and after). Modes use this for client registry operations and to
    close listen sockets in interrupt_fn.




Returns


Pointer to TCP server, or NULL if not initialized


Definition at line 252 of file server_like.c.


252                                                        {
253   return g_tcp_server_initialized ? &g_tcp_server : NULL;
254 }






Referenced by acds_server_init().






nat_upnp_context_t * session_server_like_get_upnp_ctx
  (void )


Get the UPnP context owned by server_like.




Available after UPnP mapping completes (i.e., during init_fn and
    after). Use for querying the public address.




Returns


Pointer to UPnP context, or NULL if not initialized or
  disabled


Definition at line 260 of file server_like.c.


260                                                            {
261   return g_upnp_ctx;
262 }










websocket_server_t *
  session_server_like_get_websocket_server (void )


Get the WebSocket server instance owned by server_like.




Available after WebSocket init completes (i.e., during init_fn and
    after).




Returns


Pointer to WebSocket server, or NULL if not initialized
  or disabled


Definition at line 264 of file server_like.c.


264                                                                    {
265   return g_websocket_thread_started ? &g_websocket_server : NULL;
266 }










asciichat_error_t session_server_like_handshake
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t *
  transport)


Perform the server-side crypto handshake on a transport.




Runs the full handshake sequence: receive PROTOCOL_VERSION, send
    parameters, key exchange, auth challenge, and completion. Both server and
    discovery-service modes use this single implementation.




The caller must initialize the handshake context
    (crypto_handshake_init or crypto_handshake_init_with_password) and configure
    keys/whitelist on it before calling this function.




Parameters


ctx Initialized handshake context with keys/auth
  configured


transport ACIP transport for the client connection


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on handshake
  failure


Definition at line 305 of file server_like.c.


305                                                                                                               {
306   if (!ctx || !transport) {
307     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "handshake context or transport is NULL");
308   }
309 
310   asciichat_error_t result;
311 
312   // Step 0: Receive PROTOCOL_VERSION from client
313   {
314     packet_type_t pkt_type;
315     void *pkt_data = NULL;
316     size_t pkt_len = 0;
317     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
318 
319     result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
320     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
321       log_warn("Failed to receive PROTOCOL_VERSION from client");
322       return result;
323     }
324 
325     if (pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
326       log_warn("Expected PROTOCOL_VERSION, got packet type %u", pkt_type);
327       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
328       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected PROTOCOL_VERSION packet");
329     }
330 
331     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
332     log_debug("Received PROTOCOL_VERSION from client");
333   }
334 
335   // Step 1: Send crypto parameters
336   result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(ctx, transport);
337   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
338     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed");
339     return result;
340   }
341 
342   // Step 2: Key exchange (send server key)
343   result = crypto_handshake_server_start(ctx, transport);
344   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
345     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed");
346     return result;
347   }
348 
349   // Step 3: Receive client key exchange response, send auth challenge
350   {
351     packet_type_t pkt_type;
352     void *pkt_data = NULL;
353     size_t pkt_len = 0;
354     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
355 
356     result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
357     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
358       log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE");
359       return result;
360     }
361 
362     result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(ctx, transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
363     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
364 
365     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
366       log_warn("Crypto auth challenge failed");
367       return result;
368     }
369   }
370 
371   // Step 4: Complete handshake (skip if already complete from auth challenge)
372   if (ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
373     packet_type_t pkt_type;
374     void *pkt_data = NULL;
375     size_t pkt_len = 0;
376     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
377 
378     result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
379     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
380       log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE");
381       return result;
382     }
383 
384     result = crypto_handshake_server_complete(ctx, transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
385     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
386 
387     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
388       log_warn("Crypto handshake completion failed");
389       return result;
390     }
391   }
392 
393   log_info("Crypto handshake complete");
394   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
395 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, buffer_pool_free(),
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, packet_receive_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, SET_ERRNO, and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), and
    acds_websocket_client_handler().






asciichat_error_t session_server_like_mdns_advertise
  (const char * name, const char * type, uint16_t port, const char **
  txt, size_t txt_count)


Advertise or re-advertise an mDNS service.




Convenience wrapper for modes that need deferred or updated
    advertisement (e.g., server mode advertises after ACDS registration provides
    session string).




Parameters


name Service instance name


type Service type (e.g., "_ascii-chat._tcp")


port Service port


txt Optional TXT records (NULL-terminated array), or NULL


txt_count Number of TXT records


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error if mDNS is not
  initialized


Definition at line 272 of file server_like.c.


273                                                                                          {
274   if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
275     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "mDNS context not initialized");
276   }
277 
278   char hostname[256] = {0};
279   gethostname(hostname, sizeof(hostname) - 1);
280 
281   asciichat_mdns_service_t service = {
282       .name = name,
283       .type = type,
284       .host = hostname,
285       .port = port,
286       .txt_records = txt,
287       .txt_count = txt_count,
288   };
289 
290   asciichat_error_t result = asciichat_mdns_advertise(g_mdns_ctx, &service);
291   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
292     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to advertise mDNS service");
293     log_warn("mDNS advertising failed - LAN discovery disabled");
294   } else {
295     log_info("mDNS: Service advertised as '%s' type '%s' (port=%d)", name, type, port);
296   }
297 
298   return result;
299 }






References asciichat_mdns_advertise(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_info,
    log_warn, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by session_server_like_run().






asciichat_error_t session_server_like_run (const
  session_server_like_config_t * config)


Run a server-like mode with unified lifecycle management.




Orchestrates the complete lifecycle:








Initialization
  Sequence



  
1.

  
Keepawake validation and setup

  
2.

  
File descriptor limit raising (if configured)

  
3.

  
Signal handler registration (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGPIPE)

  
4.

  
TCP server init (binds to GET_OPTION port/address)

  
5.

  
UPnP port mapping (if enabled)

  
6.

  
mDNS context init and optional immediate advertisement

  
7.

  
WebSocket server init and event loop thread (if enabled)

  
8.

  
Mode-specific init_fn()

  
9.

  
Status screen and keyboard threads (if status_fn provided)

  
10.

  
TCP accept loop (blocks until shutdown)








Cleanup
  (always runs, in order)



  
11.

  
Join status screen and keyboard threads

  
12.

  
Stop WebSocket server (atomic stop, cancel, join with 500ms timeout)

  
13.

  
Mode-specific cleanup_fn() (TCP registry still available)

  
14.

  
Destroy WebSocket server

  
15.

  
Destroy TCP server

  
16.

  
Close UPnP port mapping

  
17.

  
Destroy mDNS context

  
18.

  
Disable keepawake




Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration
  (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or first error from init or
  accept loop


Definition at line 401 of file server_like.c.


401                                                                                       {
402   if (!config) {
403     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
404   }
405   if (!config->init_fn) {
406     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "init_fn is NULL");
407   }
408   if (!config->interrupt_fn) {
409     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "interrupt_fn is NULL");
410   }
411   if (!config->tcp_handler) {
412     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "tcp_handler is NULL");
413   }
414 
415   g_config = config;
416   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
417 
418   /* === 1. Keepawake === */
419 
420   bool keepawake_enabled = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
421   bool keepawake_disabled = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
422   if (keepawake_enabled && keepawake_disabled) {
423     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot specify both --keepawake and --no-keepawake");
424   }
425   if (keepawake_enabled) {
426     platform_enable_keepawake();
427   }
428 
429   /* === 2. File descriptor limit === */
430 
431   if (config->raise_fd_limit) {
432     platform_raise_fd_limit(config->fd_limit_target);
433   }
434 
435   /* === 3. Signal handlers === */
436 
437   g_interrupt_fn = config->interrupt_fn;
438   platform_signal(SIGINT, (signal_handler_t)server_like_signal_handler);
439   platform_signal(SIGTERM, (signal_handler_t)server_like_signal_handler);
440 #ifndef _WIN32
441   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
442 #endif
443 
444   /* === 4. TCP server init === */
445 
446   int port = GET_OPTION(port);
447   const char *address = GET_OPTION(address);
448   const char *address6 = GET_OPTION(address6);
449 
450   bool ipv4_has_value = (address && strlen(address) > 0);
451   bool ipv6_has_value = (address6 && strlen(address6) > 0);
452 
453   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
454       .port = port,
455       .ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? address : NULL,
456       .ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? address6 : NULL,
457       .bind_ipv4 = ipv4_has_value || !ipv6_has_value,
458       .bind_ipv6 = ipv6_has_value || !ipv4_has_value,
459       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
460       .client_handler = config->tcp_handler,
461       .user_data = config->tcp_user_data,
462       .status_update_fn = NULL,
463       .status_update_data = NULL,
464   };
465 
466   result = tcp_server_init(&g_tcp_server, &tcp_config);
467   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
468     log_error("Failed to initialize TCP server on port %d", port);
469     goto cleanup;
470   }
471   g_tcp_server_initialized = true;
472   log_info("TCP server initialized on port %d", port);
473 
474   /* === 5. UPnP port mapping === */
475 
476   if (config->upnp.enabled && GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
477     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, config->upnp.description, &g_upnp_ctx);
478 
479     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
480       char public_addr[22];
481       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
482         char msg[256];
483         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
484         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
485         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
486       }
487     } else {
488       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
489     }
490   }
491 
492   /* === 6. mDNS === */
493 
494   if (config->mdns.enabled) {
495     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
496     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
497       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
498       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN discovery will not be available");
499     } else if (config->mdns.service_name) {
500       // Immediate advertisement if service_name is provided
501       session_server_like_mdns_advertise(config->mdns.service_name, config->mdns.service_type, port, NULL, 0);
502     }
503   }
504 
505   /* === 7. WebSocket server === */
506 
507   if (config->websocket.enabled) {
508     websocket_server_config_t ws_config = {
509         .port = GET_OPTION(websocket_port),
510         .client_handler = config->websocket.handler,
511         .user_data = config->websocket.user_data,
512         .tls_cert_path = GET_OPTION(websocket_tls_cert),
513         .tls_key_path = GET_OPTION(websocket_tls_key),
514     };
515 
516     memset(&g_websocket_server, 0, sizeof(g_websocket_server));
517     asciichat_error_t ws_result = websocket_server_init(&g_websocket_server, &ws_config);
518     if (ws_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
519       log_warn("Failed to initialize WebSocket server - browser clients will not be supported");
520     } else {
521       log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d", GET_OPTION(websocket_port));
522       if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_thread, "websocket_event_loop", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
523                                   &g_websocket_server) == 0) {
524         g_websocket_thread_started = true;
525         log_info("WebSocket event loop thread started");
526       } else {
527         log_error("Failed to start WebSocket event loop thread");
528       }
529     }
530   }
531 
532   /* === 8. Mode-specific init === */
533 
534   result = config->init_fn(config->init_user_data);
535   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
536     log_error("Mode initialization failed");
537     goto cleanup;
538   }
539 
540   /* === 9. Status screen === */
541 
542   if (config->status_fn) {
543     bool status_opt = GET_OPTION(status_screen);
544     bool status_explicit = GET_OPTION(status_screen_explicitly_set);
545     if ((status_opt && terminal_is_interactive()) || (status_explicit && status_opt)) {
546       atomic_store_bool(&g_status_should_exit, false);
547       ui_status_log_init();
548 
549       if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread_func, NULL) == 0) {
550         g_status_screen_thread_started = true;
551         log_info("Status screen thread started");
552       } else {
553         log_error("Failed to start status screen thread");
554         ui_status_log_destroy();
555       }
556     }
557   }
558 
559   /* === 10. TCP accept loop (blocks) === */
560 
561   log_info("Server accepting connections on port %d", port);
562   result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
563   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
564     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
565   }
566 
567   /* === CLEANUP === */
568 
569 cleanup:
570   g_config = NULL;
571 
572   /* 11. Stop status screen */
573   if (g_status_screen_thread_started) {
574     atomic_store_bool(&g_status_should_exit, true);
575     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, 2000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
576     if (join_result != 0) {
577       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit within 2s");
578     }
579     g_status_screen_thread_started = false;
580     ui_status_log_destroy();
581   }
582 
583   /* 12. Stop WebSocket server */
584   if (g_websocket_thread_started) {
585     atomic_store_bool(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
586     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
587     int ws_join = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
588     if (ws_join != 0) {
589       log_warn("WebSocket event loop thread did not exit within 500ms");
590     }
591     g_websocket_thread_started = false;
592   }
593 
594   /* 13. Mode-specific cleanup */
595   if (config->cleanup_fn) {
596     config->cleanup_fn(config->cleanup_user_data);
597   }
598 
599   /* 14. Destroy WebSocket server */
600   if (config->websocket.enabled) {
601     websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
602     log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
603   }
604 
605   /* 15. Destroy TCP server */
606   if (g_tcp_server_initialized) {
607     tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
608     g_tcp_server_initialized = false;
609     log_debug("TCP server destroyed");
610   }
611 
612   /* 16. UPnP cleanup */
613   if (g_upnp_ctx) {
614     nat_upnp_close(&g_upnp_ctx);
615     log_debug("UPnP port mapping closed");
616   }
617 
618   /* 17. mDNS cleanup */
619   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
620     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
621     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
622     log_debug("mDNS context destroyed");
623   }
624 
625   /* 18. Keepawake */
626   if (keepawake_enabled) {
627     platform_disable_keepawake();
628   }
629 
630   return result;
631 }






References asciichat_mdns_destroy(),
    asciichat_mdns_init(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_create, asciichat_thread_join_timeout(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_server_like_config::cleanup_fn,
    session_server_like_config::cleanup_user_data,
    session_server_like_config::description,
    session_server_like_config::enabled, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    session_server_like_config::fd_limit_target, GET_OPTION,
    session_server_like_config::handler,
    session_server_like_config::init_fn,
    session_server_like_config::init_user_data,
    session_server_like_config::interrupt_fn, log_console,
    log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    LOG_INFO, log_info, log_warn,
    session_server_like_config::mdns, nat_upnp_close(),
    nat_upnp_get_address(), nat_upnp_open(), NS_PER_MS_INT,
    platform_disable_keepawake(), platform_enable_keepawake(),
    platform_raise_fd_limit(), platform_signal(),
    session_server_like_config::raise_fd_limit, safe_snprintf(),
    session_server_like_config::service_name,
    session_server_like_config::service_type,
    session_server_like_mdns_advertise(), SET_ERRNO,
    session_server_like_config::status_fn,
    session_server_like_config::tcp_handler, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), tcp_server_run(),
    session_server_like_config::tcp_user_data,
    terminal_is_interactive(), ui_status_log_destroy(),
    ui_status_log_init(), session_server_like_config::upnp,
    session_server_like_config::user_data,
    session_server_like_config::websocket,
    websocket_server_cancel_service(), websocket_server_destroy(),
    and websocket_server_init().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.11.0
    ascii-chat
